page: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge is a graphics-intensive, high-resource server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, the instance bundles four NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 179 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and network connectivity is anchored by a 200 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data highlights the instance's top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing and machine learning tasks. The g6e.24xlarge achieves top-tier (top 10%) performance in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark CPU encryption. LLM inference benchmarks are exceptionally strong, placing in the top 10% for both prompt processing and text generation across small (135M), medium (7B), and large (70B) models. Single-core performance and memory bandwidth for small cached blocks rank in the top 25%, while large-block uncached memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the average tier.

Given its dense hardware profile—integrating high-capacity NVMe storage, a massive DDR4 memory footprint, and four enterprise-grade GPUs—the g6e.24xlarge offers high qualitative resource density. The tradeoff lies in its specialized architecture, which is highly optimized for parallel processing but may represent excess capacity for standard non-GPU workloads. This instance is highly suited for large-scale LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, ray tracing, database operations, and high-throughput multi-threaded computing.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge is a high-performance, GPU-accelerated server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The hardware configuration includes four NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM, 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, cryptographic operations, and LLM inference processing across various model sizes. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in ray tracing, software compilation, and database operations. The g6e.24xlarge is highly suited for demanding workloads such as large-scale machine learning inference, complex graphics rendering, high-throughput database management, and parallel scientific computing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 179 GB VRAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.24xlarge features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, four NVIDIA L40S GPUs, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for intensive GPU workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU and memory-optimized platform featuring four enterprise accelerators and high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 7R13 vCPUs and 768 GB of DDR4 memory.
Four NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs with 179 GB of total VRAM.
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and multi-core CPU benchmarks.
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Optimized for machine learning, ray tracing, and database operations.
